title: Off-target Effects
summary: Off-target effects occur when a Cas-guide complex edits genomic sites that are similar, but not identical, to the intended target sequence.

sections.heading: Specificity determinants
sections.content: Off-target cleavage depends on mismatch number, mismatch position, PAM compatibility, bulges, chromatin accessibility, guide concentration, and exposure duration. PAM-proximal mismatches are often more disruptive than PAM-distal mismatches.

sections.heading: Risk assessment
sections.content: Early risk screening checks for extreme GC content, repetitive sequence, low complexity, and close genomic matches. Production workflows should use genome-wide alignment or validated specificity assays when safety or interpretability matters.

key_points: Low-complexity guides can increase ambiguity during genome-wide matching.
High-specificity Cas variants and careful guide selection can reduce off-target risk.
limitations: Simple heuristics identify obvious risk patterns but cannot quantify genome-wide specificity without reference genome search and experimental confirmation.
